author: Wendy the Food Scientist
title: Crispy Lentil Crackers: No Oil, All Crunch
one_liner: Make high-protein, high-fiber lentil crackers using just lentils, water, and spices — no oil needed.
key_points: Soaked lentils blended smooth form the base — starch gelatinizes then dehydrates in the oven for crispiness | Spread batter as thin as possible, bake twice: 20 min first, then 30-35 min after slicing | Fully customizable — use any lentil, seeds, herbs, or spices | Cheaper and healthier alternative to store-bought protein snacks
transcript: How to make high-fiber, high-protein lentil crackers. They turn out super crispy without any frying, all thanks to the starches in lentils. It's so easy to make, you never have to buy expensive protein snacks again. You can choose any lentil, herbs and spices you like. The trick is to spread it thin, bake until set but soft enough to slice. Then bake again until super crispy.
recipe_ingredients: 125g / ⅔ cup dry lentils | 100ml / ⅓ cup + 1 tbsp water | 30g / 3 tbsp sesame seeds | ½ tsp salt | ¼ tsp dried rosemary
recipe_instructions: Soak lentils in cold water for 8–12 hours, then drain and rinse well. | Blend soaked lentils and water in a high-speed blender until completely smooth. | Stir in sesame seeds, salt, and rosemary. | Spread mixture as thinly as possible onto a lined baking tray. | Bake at 180°C / 355°F for 20 minutes. | Remove from oven and cut into squares or rectangles. | Return to oven and bake for a further 30–35 minutes until golden, dry, and crispy. | Allow to cool completely before serving — they crisp up further as they cool.

author: Wendy the Food Scientist
title: Soak Nuts for the Crispiest Granola Ever
one_liner: Soaking nuts and seeds before baking removes bitter tannins and creates a crispier, more porous granola texture.
key_points: Soaking leaches bitter tannins (especially from walnuts and sunflower seeds), resulting in a cleaner, smoother flavor | Nuts swell during soaking, then become more porous and brittle after drying — making granola extra crispy | Soak times vary: almonds 8–12h, walnuts/pecans/pumpkin/sunflower seeds 4–6h, cashews 2–4h; always use salted water (1.5–2 tsp per liter) | Bake soaked nuts at 130°C (265°F) for 30–40 min to dry, then 175°C (350°F) for 15–20 min to roast
transcript: This is my secret to the best granola you've ever tasted. Perfectly crispy and tasty in 5 simple steps. Rinse your nuts and seeds then soak them to draw out soluble bitter compounds like tannins. After soaking, rinse again to wash away what's been released. Then bake at low temperature until dry to the touch. Lastly, add the rest of the granola ingredients and bake until golden. It gets even crispier as it cools when you can hear it crackling in the pan. Most recipes skip the soaking step but trust me, this is what takes your granola to the next level.
recipe_ingredients: nuts and seeds of choice (almonds, walnuts, pecans, pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, cashews, sesame seeds) | 1.5–2 tsp salt per 1 liter water (for soaking) | remaining granola ingredients (oats, sweetener, oil, etc.) as desired
recipe_instructions: 1. Rinse nuts and seeds thoroughly | 2. Soak in salted water (1.5–2 tsp salt per liter): almonds 8–12h, walnuts/pecans/pumpkin/sunflower seeds 4–6h, cashews 2–4h, sesame seeds 1–2h (optional) | 3. Rinse soaked nuts and seeds again to wash away released tannins | 4. Spread on a baking tray and bake at 130°C (265°F) for 30–40 minutes until dry to the touch | 5. Add remaining granola ingredients, then bake at 175°C (350°F) for 15–20 minutes until golden | 6. Let cool in the pan — granola will crackle and crisp further as it cools

author: Keto Snackz
title: High-Protein Blueberry Cheesecake
one_liner: A 6-ingredient baked blueberry cheesecake using cottage cheese for extra protein.
key_points: Blend cottage cheese, cream cheese, egg, sweetener, and vanilla; pour into a greased dish | Add blueberries on top and bake at 325°F for 30–35 minutes | Chill for a couple hours before serving | Pro tip: mash extra blueberries and swirl on top with a toothpick
transcript: If you bake blueberries with cottage cheese, an egg, cream cheese, and sweetener for 30 minutes, you get a healthy blueberry cheesecake.
recipe_ingredients: 3 tbsp cottage cheese | 2 oz cream cheese | 1 large egg | 2 tbsp sweetener (monk fruit) | 2 tbsp blueberries | 1 tsp vanilla extract
recipe_instructions: Blend all ingredients except the blueberries and pour into a greased dish. | Add blueberries on top and bake at 325°F for 30–35 minutes. | Take out, chill for a couple hours, and serve. | Optional: mash extra blueberries and swirl on top with a toothpick before baking.

author: Brycen Wood
title: One File Gets Your Business Into ChatGPT
one_liner: A llms.txt file on your website can get ChatGPT to recommend your business for free.
key_points: llms.txt tells AI assistants your business name, location, and differentiators | Takes ~5 minutes to create and requires no ad spend | Creator's site grew from 7 to 150 daily visitors after an AI-assisted rebuild | Most small businesses don't know this file format exists
transcript: ChatGPT is recommending my business to people who are looking for vehicle wraps. I didn't pay for that, I didn't ask for it, I just set it up with one file. Who does the best vehicle wraps in Utah? See that? That's not an ad, that's just ChatGPT telling a real potential customer to come to my shop. This is a text file that sits on my website. This file tells AI Assistants what my business is, where we're located, what we specialize in, and what sets us apart from other wrap companies. Most business owners don't even know that this file exists. But here's the thing, it only took me 5 minutes to set up. My website also went from 7 visitors a day to 150 after I rebuilt it. Two weeks, zero ads, all built on clawed code. I'll break down exactly how in another video.
